Scenario.title: KV Botnet Communications Disruption
Scenario.overview: Communications Industry Complex A sophisticated botnet campaign targeting communications infrastructure leverages supply chain compromises, default credentials, and encrypted channels to disrupt network operations while evading detection.

threat_actor_profile.motivation: Disrupt communication services for financial gain

Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Process, Target System Artifact, Command, File, Windows Registry, Malware Artifact, Network Traffic
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Reg.exe Utility, Compromise Software Supply Chain, Command Execution (Mechanism), File Access (Mechanism), Windows Registry Key Access (Mechanism), PowerShell Script, File Modification (Mechanism), Encrypted Channel, Network Traffic Content (Mechanism)

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attacker uses Reg.exe to monitor network traffic on SOHO devices, identifying vulnerable systems with exposed credentials and weak configurations.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.description: A Raspberry Pi is repurposed as a command-and-control server, with malware preloaded during supply chain compromise to establish persistent access.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.capec: CAPEC-70: Try Common or Default Usernames and Passwords
stage.description: Unpatched routers and switches with default credentials are exploited to gain initial access to the network perimeter.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC-9: Buffer Overflow in Local Command-Line Utilities
stage.description: Custom firmware is installed via Telnet/SSH, leveraging buffer overflow vulnerabilities in command-line tools to execute arbitrary code.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: Malware is configured to persist across reboots by modifying trust relationships in command execution chains.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.description: System logs are disabled or altered to erase evidence of lateral movement and command execution activities.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.capec: CAPEC-226: Session Credential Falsification through Manipulation
stage.description: Admin credentials are harvested from registry keys to maintain access across compromised devices.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: Further network sniffing identifies additional devices on the local subnet for targeted exploitation.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.description: SMB protocols are exploited using downgrade attacks to move laterally between compromised endpoints.

stage.name: Collection
stage.capec: CAPEC-675: Retrieve Data from Decommissioned Devices
stage.description: Email archives are exfiltrated from compromised endpoints before being deleted to obscure forensic analysis.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: Data is transferred to the C2 server via encrypted channels to evade network monitoring tools.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: Amplified traffic floods network hardware, causing denial-of-service conditions and disrupting communication services.

Scenario.summary: The KV Botnet campaign exploits default credentials, supply chain weaknesses, and network vulnerabilities to compromise communications infrastructure, enabling data theft and service disruption through multi-stage attacks.
